'''Joe Arundel''' (born 22 August 1991) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]] and the [[England Knights]] at the International level.

He has played for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]], spending time as a loan from Castleford at [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]] in the [[2009 RFL Championship|Co-operative Championship]]. Arundel moved to [[Hull F.C.]] in the top flight, and spent time away from [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] at the [[York City Knights]], [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and at the [[Bradford Bulls]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the Super League. A permanent move to [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] followed, with loan moves away at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[2018 RFL Championship]] and the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in [[2019 RFL League 1|League 1]].

==Background==
Arundel was born in Leeds, [[West Yorkshire, England]].

==Career==
His amateur club was [[National Conference League|Castleford Panthers]] and he has played professionally for the [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], the [[York City Knights]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), the [[Bradford Bulls]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity (Wildcats)]] (two spells, the first one on [[Loan (sports)|loan]]) in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Castleford RLFC A to Z Player List (All Time)">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/alltime.php|title=Castleford RLFC A to Z Player List (All Time)|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120216084010/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/alltime.php|archivedate=16 February 2012}}</ref><ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=933|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|url-status=unfit|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120216084416/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=933|archivedate=16 February 2012}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
Arundel made his debut for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the Super League game against [[Huddersfield Giants]] in 2008 Aged 16.<ref name="Castleford hand Arundel contract">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/castleford/7629608.stm
|title=Castleford hand Arundel contract|publisher=BBC|date=22 September 2008|access-date=29 March 2010}}</ref>

Arundel played in nine games for Castleford in 2010 and impressed scoring 2 tries. He was handed the number 4 jersey for the 2011 season.

===Bradford Bulls===
On 25 April 2014, Arundel signed for the [[Bradford Bulls]] for the rest of the season on loan along with [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] teammate [[Jay Pitts]].

A goal kicking Centre, Arundel is excellent in defence and has a tremendous work rate<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30066/bulls-pick-up-pitts-and |title=Bulls pick up Pitts and Arundel |publisher=RUGBY-LEAGUE.COM |accessdate=18 February 2017 |date=25 April 2014 |archivedate=26 April 2014 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140426232152/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30066/bulls-pick-up-pitts-and |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-FC-send-Joe-Arundel-Jay-Pitts-Bradford-Bulls/story-21013179-detail/story.html|title=Hull FC send Joe Arundel and Jay Pitts to Bradford Bulls on loan until the end of the season|date=25 April 2014|newspaper=Hull Daily Mail|accessdate=18 February 2017}}</ref>

===Halifax Panthers===
On 5 November 2021, it was reported that he had signed a part-time playing contract with [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 Nov 2021|accessdate=7 Nov 2021|title=Joe Arundel decides to go part-time with Championship club|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/joe-arundel-joins-championship-halifax-panthers/}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 1 January 2023, Arundel signed a contract to re-join one of his former clubs Bradford for the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/ex-wakefield-trinity-hull-fc-and-castleford-tigers-star-joins-bradford-bulls-3971118/|title=Ex-Wakefield Trinity, Hull FC and Castleford Tigers star joins Bradford Bulls|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>

===Batley Bulldogs===
On 24 October 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=24 Oct 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Ex-Castleford Tigers, Wakefield Trinity, Hull FC and Bradford Bulls centre Joe Arundel finds new club|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-castleford-tigers-wakefield-trinity-hull-fc-and-bradford-bulls-centre-joe-arundel-finds-new-club/}}</ref>

On 20 November 2025 it was confirmed that he was one of 6 players to leave [[Batley Bulldogs]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Nov 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Super League duo among sextet departing Championship club|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/batley-release-list-end-of-2025}}</ref>
